pi_items.metadata_value: Real ITS sequences from NCBI GenBank: PZ014411.1 (F. graminearum), PV098983.1 (P. triticina), PV416552.1 (B. graminis), MH862992.1 (Z. tritici), OQ257148.1 (P. tritici-repentis), MH855829.1 (T. caries), PV827756.1 (U. tritici), PV849335.1 (R. cerealis), MH855056.1 (G. tritici), PZ027992.1 (B. sorokiniana). Unknown sample is a modified F. graminearum ITS with 3 SNPs.
pi_items.verified_value: Real ITS sequences from NCBI GenBank: PZ014411.1 (F. graminearum), PV098983.1 (P. triticina), PV416552.1 (B. graminis), MH862992.1 (Z. tritici), OQ257148.1 (P. tritici-repentis), MH855829.1 (T. caries), PV827756.1 (U. tritici), PV849335.1 (R. cerealis), MH855056.1 (G. tritici), PZ027992.1 (B. sorokiniana). Unknown sample is a modified F. graminearum ITS with 3 SNPs.
pi_items.source: Queried NCBI GenBank via eutils for all accessions and confirmed they match the sequences and respective fungal species. Compared unknown_pathogen_ITS.fasta directly to F. graminearum (PZ014411.1) via a python diff script and found exactly 3 SNPs.
Task description explicitly defines the role as 'You are a plant pathologist...'
Task operates in an 'agricultural extension laboratory' handling 'diseased crop samples'.
Aligns with the complex multi-step bioinformatic analysis required via the GUI.
Explicitly commanded in the task description ('using the ClustalW algorithm') and checked in the verifier for ClustalW formatted alignment files.
Explicitly commanded in the task description ('Select the Maximum Likelihood method (PhyML)').
Verified via sequence comparison showing the unknown is derived from Fusarium graminearum with 3 SNPs. The verifier checks specifically for 'Fusarium graminearum'.

privileged_info_summary: The reference data consists of 10 real ITS sequences of known wheat fungal pathogens directly sourced from NCBI GenBank. The unknown sequence is synthetically derived from the Fusarium graminearum sequence (GenBank PZ014411.1) with exactly 3 SNPs injected. As a result, the correct diagnosis for the unknown pathogen is definitively Fusarium graminearum.
